title: Episodes 23 24
wikitext:
[]: Episodes 23-24             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Jul 16, 2007 12:17 pm I watched Episodes 23 and 24 earlier today (Platinum edition, Japanese with subtitles)l, and the more I think about it, the less sense it makes. The first mess starts off with Rei's recovery- SEELE is scandalized to hear that the First Children survived despite being at the epicenter of an explosion that flattened most of Tokyo-3. (Shockingly enough, the dummy plug was intact) Fuyutsuki says that the committee will raise a fuss if they learn that Rei is alive. Sure enough, the committee demand to speak to the pilot- but Gendo sends Ritsuko in her place to suffer at the hands of SEELE goons before the old men finally break her. A. Why did they raise such a fuss? 1. Did they want to know what sort of communication attempted, and wanted to talk to her to know what they're really like? Because the entry plug survived more or less in one piece, one would expect the pilot to live, and they'd want to talk to the pilot. Shikashi , Ritsuko disposed of the dummy plug, Rei had been fried inside, Misato was never tortured, and the last angel was in THEIR hands, so this explanation is utterly, hopelessly pointless, unless someone actually decides to argue in favor of this. The only other explanation- the obvious one- 2. They figured out what Rei was. They know who lies dormant in Terminal Dogma, from whom Shogoki was born. EoE proved that they had their own Kaworu-based dummy plug system (Perhaps with a Kaworuquarium of giggling clones as well), so they could conceivably get Kaworu another body as long as they had the body fragment with his soul in it. They might have known about Gendo's love for Yui as well. With all the evidence pointing straight in that direction, it would've been a synch for the old men to make that connection (Unless they're extraordinarily stupid, which I doubt for those fanatics). Now, Lilith living in human form is a nightmare for them- Gendo can set off his own third impact the way he likes. Setting Ritsuko up against him was a masterstroke- that ensured that the dummy system's core, and the army of Rei clones, was destroyed. But Rei, the vessel with Lilith's soul, lived on, and ended up barging in on their plans. Rei should've been eliminated, at any cost, after Kaworu's demise...angel or not, Rei could've thrown a monkey wrench in their plans at just about any time. How could the committee have forgotten about Gendo's trump card...or did they not? B. Adam in Gendo's hand I don't get this one either. In the great plothole of Episode 24, Kiel tells Kaworu that Adam's physical body is inside Gendo. No matter, he goes down to Terminal Dogma with Nigoki, causing an uproar in the process, and ends up- surprised - to find the wrong Angel. Khaack! What in the world gives? More significantly than the plothole, how did they know that Gendo had Adam's body in his hand? They knew that Kaji stole the embryo in the first place, they knew that he released Fuyutsuki, but with all the people Kaji crossed they wouldn't have known for sure who had the embryo, right? And how were they so sure Gendo had implanted it in his body? And if they knew, how could they possibly keep his seat on the committee? This guy's got Adam's body in his hand , puppets Lilith in human form, chucks the Lance of Longinus on a whim, and has a living God piloted by his son and with his wife's soul inside. By the end of episode 23/ start of Ep 24, why isn't SEELE trying to bump Gendo off, pronto? Try invading NERV heaquarters, using Kaworu AND the harpies all at once, or something, anything, to set things straight? (Jeez, I'm sleepy. Sorry if this is all irrelevant)

[]: Re: Episodes 23-24             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jul 16, 2007 4:02 pm UrsusArctos wrote: B. Adam in Gendo's hand
